subject: Geography
title: Cave Bacteria and Extremophiles
description: A micro-lesson that makes Cave Bacteria and Extremophiles usable.

hook.text: In Romania's Movile Cave, bacteria live on hydrogen sulfide gas—no sunlight, no photosynthesis.

buildup.text: The cave was sealed for 5.5 million years before workers accidentally broke through in 1986.

discovery.text: Inside, 48 species were found—33 of them completely new to science.

twist.text: The air is toxic to humans but sustains a full ecosystem powered by chemical energy.

climax.text: If life thrives here without sunlight, it could survive in similar conditions on other planets.

punchline.text: Life doesn't need the sun. It just needs chemistry.

quiz.question: How long was Movile Cave sealed before its discovery?
